danger_signals.visual_form: Numeric fuel counter (100 to 0) at bottom right of screen decreasing while in jet mode
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent crash and life loss if jet remains airborne without refueling

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual cue on screen center top below score indicating safe reversion altitude
danger_signals.meaning: Flying too high prevents transformation back to robot; flying too low risks crash even over ground
Jet crash over ocean results in immediate life loss (no ground to revert to)

danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen enemy attacks and environmental hazards
danger_signals.meaning: One-hit death mechanic; any collision is fatal

danger_signals.visual_form: Animation sequence during robot-to-jet or jet-to-robot conversion
danger_signals.meaning: Player cannot shoot during transformation; window of vulnerability to enemy fire

safety_signals.visual_form: White sphere emitting electricity on ground terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Restores jet fuel; safe to collect in robot mode before transforming

safety_signals.visual_form: Solid terrain (grassland, desert, space ground) visible below player
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for robot mode; can revert from jet to avoid crashes

safety_signals.visual_form: Aerial battleground with safety from fuel depletion
safety_signals.meaning: During boss encounters, fuel does not deplete; player can focus on combat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Robot Ixpel transforming into jet fighter
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Earth (grassland, desert) and artificial space station ground

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Boss entities (STV on Earth; main alien superweapon in space)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple smaller enemy types with varied attack patterns across game progression
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Outer space environment; dense enemy formations in later levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mountainous grassland terrain; standard enemy density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player navigates mixed robot/jet platforming; can revert to robot on ground
Similar mechanics with visual environment swap indicating progression
Mandatory jet transformation; jet crash over water = instant death; requires fuel management

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss entity in aerial space over ocean at desert's end
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aerial combat with Big-Bang charged weapon required; fuel does not deplete during fight

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bottomless sky environment after ground phase; alien superweapon visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single long ground phase then transition to space; no fuel depletion concern in aerial portions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Main antagonist entity in space environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final aerial combat; Big-Bang required to defeat; no crash risk or fuel drain

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Return to Earth grassland; visual terrain reset
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New enemy patterns and increased difficulty; no narrative ending

environmental_storytelling.technique: Progressive environment design signaling narrative arc
environmental_storytelling.example: Journey from Earth (grassland → desert → ocean) establishes human territory under threat; progression to space establishes alien origin point of invasion
Ocean terrain forces jet transformation, establishing spatial/mechanical logic that jet is necessary for overwater travel and connecting gameplay to narrative threat
STV boss positioned at Earth's boundary (ocean/coast); alien superweapon in space establishes invasion hierarchy and two-theater conflict

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can move forward/backward and jump; cannot fly; fires front/diagonal shots
Can travel anywhere on screen; fuel depletes over time; limited altitude zone for safe reversion; fires front/diagonal shots relative to direction
Becomes vulnerable; cannot shoot; susceptible to one-hit death
Defeated by Pulse Laser standard shots; multiple patterns across difficulty loops
Requires Big-Bang charged weapon to defeat; cannot be killed with Pulse Laser alone
Stationary; emits electrical visual effect; restores jet fuel to full

notes: Formation Z communicates world-state primarily through environmental progression (Earth terrain → space) and mechanical affordances (fuel system, altitude restrictions, transformation vulnerability). Danger signals are heavily mechanical (fuel depletion, altitude limits, transformation windows) rather than visual faction markers. The game lacks explicit faction color-coding or political context; conflict is presented as simple invasion scenario (aliens vs. human robot). Safety is communicated through fuel pickup visibility and ground presence. Boss encounters reset fuel mechanics, signaling narrative climax zones. Looping structure with pattern changes maintains threat escalation without narrative conclusion.
